content.full_text:
Court 
Dear Mr. 


WILL AM F. 
RENO 
RAYMOND 
"SIMMONS 
ROBERT O'FARRELL 
JUDGES 
FMUNICIPALCOURT 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Gentlemen: 
DIST leT 
ROBERT DOWNS 
CLERK OF MUNICIPAL COURT 
January 23, 1978 
I have examined the most recent literature with reference to campaign 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
I am a Municipal Court Judge 
for the Salinas Judicial District, County of Monterey. 
This is not a year in 
which I am engaged in an election campaign, and I further certify that I have 
not received, nor have I spent, any amount of money. 
I find it exceedingly difficult to make heads or tails out of all the infor-
mation requested. 
However, it seems to me that I would fit under the des-
cription of an elected office holder in an "off year", and, as a judge, not 
required to file semi-annual campaign statements. 
If I am incorrect, could you 
please forward to me the form so that I can tell you that I did not receive any 
money nor expend any money. 
dl 
Thank you kindly for your courtesy and cooperation. 
Very truly yours, 
F. Moreno, Presiding Judge 
Municipal Court

sections.question: This is not a year in which I am engaged in an election campaign, and I further certify that I have not received, nor have I spent, any amount of money. I find it exceedingly difficult to make heads or tails out of all the information requested. However, it seems to me that I would fit under the description of an elected office holder in an "off year", and, as a judge, not required to file semi-annual campaign statements. If I am incorrect, could you please forward to me the form so that I can tell you that I did not receive any money nor expend any money.
sections.question_synthetic: As a Municipal Court Judge in an off-election year who has received and spent no campaign funds, is the judge required to file semi-annual campaign statements under the Political Reform Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a judge to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present. The letter contains the substantive inquiry and request for guidance. Letter dated January 23, 1978.

embedding.summary: A Municipal Court Judge for the Salinas Judicial District writes to the FPPC seeking clarification on whether judges are required to file campaign disclosure statements in off-election years, stating they believe they are exempt as an elected official in an off-year with no campaign activity or funds.
